original: 105-year-old Julia Hawkins sets a world record as the first woman and first American her age to run 100 meters at Louisiana Senior Olympic Games
American fashion designer Ruth Harkness captures a panda cub (Su Lin) in China - becomes 1st live panda cub to enter the US
Russia'sGarry Kasparov, at 22, becomes the youngest World Chess Champion ever with a 13-11 win over fellow countryman Anatoly Karpov
Choi Hong Hi, South Korean army general and Founder of Taekwon-Do, born in Hwadae, Myongchon County, North Hamgyong Province, Japanese Korea (d. 2002)
Lou Ferrigno, American body builder and actor (Incredible Hulk), born in Brooklyn, New York
Video game Halo 2 a first person shooter first released on Xbox by Bungie Studios
Evander HolyfieldupsetsMike Tysonin 11th-round knockout in Las Vegas to regain WBA heavyweight boxing title; second boxer, after Muhammad Ali, to win a heavyweight title 3 times
The X-15 rocket plane under USAF Major Robert M White achieved a world record speed of 4,093 mph (Mach 6.04) and reached 101,600 feet (30,970 m or over 19 miles) altitude
East Berlin opens its borders at Checkpoint Charlie when thousands arrive after a bureaucratic error announces that restrictions on travel to the West have been lifted
Joint European Torus (JET) scientists in Culham England successfully harness nuclear fusion to produce the first large amount of controlled fusion power
Archaeologists announce discovery of oldest decipherable sentence on an ivory comb “May this tusk root out the lice of the hair and the beard.” in 1,700 B.C Canaanite script from Tel Lachish, Israel
Cheyenne Pyle, America's youngest heart transplant patient at 90 mins old, born at Jackson Children's Hospital, Miami, Florida (dies 7 days later)
False alarm of a Soviet ballistic missile attack by US NORAD system after technician fails to code a test properly
1st airplane flight to last more than 5 minutes
After a month of delays off the English coast and about two months at sea, the Mayflower spots land (Cape Cod)
The Cullinan Diamond, the largest ever discovered, is presented to KingEdward VIIon his birthday
Celebrations held in Germany to mark the 25th anniversary of the fall of the Berlin Wall; white balloons marking a stretch of the wall symbolize its disappearance
The Beatles meet future managerBrian Epsteinafter a lunch time performance at The Cavern Club in Liverpool, England
Several US states and parts of Canada are hit by a series of blackouts lasting up to 13 hours in the Northeast Blackout
2020 becomes busiest Atlantic hurricane season ever as tropical storm Theta named record 29th storm
Drugmakers BioNTech and Pfizer announce their COVID-19 vaccine is over 90% effective in a first look at the results from their phase 3 trial involving nearly 44,000 people
Venus Express mission of the European Space Agency is launched from the Baikonur Cosmodrome in Kazakhstan
Discovery flies from Vandenberg AFB to Kennedy Space Center
The 1st unmanned Saturn V rocket is launched on its first successful test flight into Earth orbit
Darmstadtium, chemical element 110, is discovered at GSI Helmholtz Centre for Heavy Ion Research near Darmstadt, Germany
San Diego's SeaWorld announces it will overhaul its killer whale show after controversy over the whales treatment
Bob Cassilly, American artist, sculptor, and founder of the St. Louis City Museum, born in Webster Groves, Missouri (d. 2011)
British Petroleum surveyors flying over Libyan desert observe wreck of WWII bomber, later identified as the lost 'Lady Be Good'
Storm "Freshwater Fury" sinks 8 ore-carriers on Great Lakes
Hurricane storm wave sweeps over Santa Cruz del Sur Cuba kills 2,500
Robert A. Millikanconfirms the existence of cosmic rays from outer space in a speech to the National Academy of Sciences at Madison, Wisconsin
Steven Lopez, American taekwondo martial artist, born in Nicaragua
Joe Cada becomes the youngest winner of the World Series of Poker
Al Capp, cartoonist of Li'l Abner, creates Sadie Hawkins Day
Karen Dotrice, British actress (Mary Poppins), born in Guernsey, Channel Islands
Pepa [Sandra Denton], Jamaican-American hip hop artist (Salt-n-Pepa) and actress, born in Kingston, Jamaica (birth date disputed)
Houston's Roman Anderson is the first NCAA player to kick 400 points
Surveyor 6 soft lands on Moon
Nobel Prize for physics awarded to American Ernest Lawrence for his invention of the cyclotron

Florence Sabin, American Scientist who was the first woman to graduate from Johns Hopkins and the first lifetime woman member of the National Academy of Sciences, born in Central City, Colorado (d. 1953)
Vietnam Veterans Memorial ("3 Servicemen") completed
Theodore Rooseveltis 1st US President to visit another country (Puerto Rico and Panama)
The Great Boston Fire of 1872. Close to 1,000 buildings destroyed
Off Cocos Island, near Sumatra, the Australian cruiser 'Sydney' sinks German cruiser 'Emden', which has been attacking ships in the Pacific
First documented Canadian football game, held at the University of Toronto
Bob Gibson, American Baseball Hall of Fame pitcher (NL Cy Young Award 1968, 70; NL MVP 1968; 9 x MLB All Star; St. Louis Cardinals), born in Omaha, Nebraska (d. 2020)
San Francisco Giants outfielderWillie Maysnamed NL MVP
American tennis starVenus Williamswins her first career year-ending title; beats Russian Vera Zvonareva 6–7, 6–0, 6–2 in the WTA Tour Championship final at Doha, Qatar
Caitlin Clarkmakes her sophomore season debut with 26 points, eight rebounds, and six assists for Iowa against New Hampshire
Larry HolmesTKOs Bonecrusher Smith in 12 for heavyweight boxing title
Matthew Bourne's innovative production of "Swan Lake" with all-male swans premieres at Sadler's Wells in London
American bass baritonePaul Robesongives the first performance at the Sydney Opera House, singing to workers from the scaffolding during construction of the building

British rockerDavid Bowieperforms on stage for the final time - three songs at a charity concert in New York City's Hammerstein Ballroom: "Wild Is the Wind", "Fantastic Voyage", and a duet of "Changes" withAlicia Keys
Joe Cocker's version of The Beatles song "With A Little Help From My Friends" becomes No. 1 single in the UK

Apple Records releases "Ringo",Ringo Starr's third studio album, in the UK - a week after its US release; his biggest commercial and critical success, featuring the singles "Photograph", "You're Sixteen", and "Oh My My"
"Bridge over Troubled Water" single recorded by Simon & Garfunkel
YouTubers KSI andLogan Paul's second boxing match; KSI wins by split decision
TV comedy "Miranda" written by and starringMiranda HartandTom Ellispremieres on BBC Two in the UK
Comedy troupe "The Goodies" make their television debut on the BBC
Hurricane hits north east US/Canada
